metadata.convention: WT baseline = standard K-12 host (MG1655 or W3110 or comparable) harbouring the standard crtEBI cassette at chromosomally integrated or plasmid-based wild-type expression, cultured in LB at 30 C with inducer on. 'increased' = lycopene titer higher than this baseline. 'decreased' = lower. 'unchanged' = within measurement noise of baseline. Fold-changes quoted in evidence are reported as published.

evidence.title: Advances in Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpression of dxs in E. coli carotenoid production was enhanced 3.5-fold
evidence.claim: Dxs OE increases lycopene ~3.5x

evidence.title: Advances in Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: mutant IDI exhibited a 1.8-fold increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: Idi OE increases lycopene ~1.8-2x

evidence.title: Advances in Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: expressed the foreign MEP pathway genes dxs and idi simultaneously in E. coli, resulting in an increase of 16.5 times in lycopene yield
evidence.claim: Combined dxs+idi OE increases lycopene ~16.5x

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering E. coli for Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J

evidence.title: Engineering the lycopene synthetic pathway: Pantoea comparison
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC
evidence.evidence_sentence: E. coli harboring the P. agglomerans crt genes produced 27 mg/l of lycopene in 2YT medium without IPTG induction, which was twofold higher than that produced by E. coli harboring the P. ananatis crt genes (12 mg/l lycopene) with 0.1 mM IPTG induction
evidence.claim: IPTG at 0.1 mM induces crt cassette expression and supports lycopene production

evidence.title: Engineering the lycopene synthetic pathway: Pantoea comparison
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC
evidence.evidence_sentence: E. coli harboring the P. agglomerans crt genes produced 27 mg/l of lycopene in 2YT medium without IPTG induction

evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC
evidence.evidence_sentence: E. coli harboring the P. agglomerans crt genes produced 27 mg/l of lycopene in 2YT medium without IPTG induction
evidence.claim: P. agglomerans crt cassette produces 27 mg/L without IPTG (constitutive leaky expression sufficient)

evidence.title: Evaluation of Various E. coli Strains for Enhanced Lycopene Production
evidence.authors: Ren J, Shen J, Thai TD, Kim M, Lee SH, Lim W, Na D
evidence.evidence_sentence: The MG1655 strain (141 mg/L) expressed the highest lycopene titer
evidence.claim: MG1655 + crtEBI + no auxiliary interventions is the WT baseline (141 mg/L)

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering E. coli for Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: transaldolase B...modulated to increase NADPH and ATP...significant increase in the lycopene yield...50.6 mg/g DCW
evidence.claim: TalB OE contributes to 50.6 mg/g DCW lycopene in combinatorial engineering

evidence.title: Evaluation of Various E. coli Strains for Enhanced Lycopene Production
evidence.authors: Ren J, Shen J, Thai TD, Kim M, Lee SH, Lim W, Na D
evidence.evidence_sentence: four E. coli genes (dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi) were cloned into a mid-copy plasmid and overexpressed
evidence.claim: dxs+dxr+ispA+idi quad-OE gives substantial lycopene boost with MG1655

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering E. coli for Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: gdhA deletion could increase the availability of NADPH
evidence.claim: ΔgdhA increases NADPH pool and lycopene
